questionText: A curve has parametric equations $x = 2t^{2}$ and $y = t - 6$. Find the gradient of the curve when $t = 1$, and state whether the curve is rising or falling there.

steps.description: Differentiate x with respect to t
steps.explanation: Start by differentiating the expression for $x$ with respect to $t$.

steps.description: Differentiate y with respect to t
steps.explanation: Differentiate $y$ in the same way to find $\dot{y}$.
The gradient of the curve is the ratio $\dfrac{\dot{y}}{\dot{x}}$.
Putting $t = 1$ into the gradient formula gives its value at that point on the curve.
A positive gradient means the curve is rising as $x$ increases through that point.
At $t = 1$ the gradient is \dfrac{1}{4} and the curve is rising there.

workedSolution.finalAnswer: At $t = 1$ the gradient is \dfrac{1}{4}, so the curve is rising there.
